keywords: self-tapping screws, drill screws, tek screws, metal screws, construction fasteners, roofing screws

body.en: Self-tapping screws (also called self-drilling or Tek screws) have a sharp point and specially designed thread that allows them to create their own mating thread in materials like sheet metal, plastic, or wood. Unlike standard screws that require pre-drilled pilot holes, self-tapping screws drill their own hole as they are driven in, significantly speeding up installation.

There are three main types: Type AB point screws have a Type A point with a finer thread, suitable for thin sheet metal up to 0.9mm. Type B point screws have a 45-degree Type B point, ideal for thicker materials up to 2.3mm. Drill point (Type III) screws have a flat tip with drill flutes, capable of drilling through steel up to 5mm without pre-drilling.

Self-tapping screws are essential in African construction: roofing installation—attaching metal roofing sheets to purlins; steel framing—connecting steel studs and tracks; HVAC ductwork—securing duct sections; solar panel mounting—fixing panel frames to racking systems; and general sheet metal fabrication for commercial buildings.

For African environments, coating selection is critical. Zinc-plated screws offer basic protection for indoor use. Black phosphate screws provide temporary protection and lubrication during installation. For outdoor and coastal applications, stainless steel (A2 304 or A4 316) or hot-dip galvanized screws are essential to prevent rusting and premature failure.

heading.en: How to Select the Right Self-Tapping Screw

body.en: Selection criteria: Material being fastened—determine thickness and type (steel, aluminum, wood). Load requirements—heavier loads require larger diameter and more threads. Environmental exposure—coastal and outdoor applications need corrosion-resistant coatings. Installation speed—drill point screws offer fastest installation through steel. Use this formula: for steel up to 2.3mm, use Type B; for steel 2.3-5mm, use Type III drill point.

q.en: What are Technical Guide and how are they used?

a.en: Technical Guide are essential components in construction and manufacturing. Proper selection depends on load requirements, environmental conditions, and material compatibility.

q.en: How do I choose the right fastener for my project?

a.en: Consider the material being fastened, load requirements, environmental exposure, and applicable standards. Consult with a professional if unsure about specifications.

q.en: What materials are available for these fasteners?

a.en: Common materials include carbon steel, stainless steel, alloy steel, and specialty alloys. Each offers different strength, corrosion resistance, and cost characteristics.
Yes, we offer custom fastener manufacturing to spec. Contact our sales team with your requirements for pricing and lead times.
